note: Cards aggregate rule-based fusion outputs computed over the current synthetic inbound scenario set.

panel: Inbound flight list / fused summary row
note: Each row summarizes scenario state plus module outputs using deterministic OCC fusion and summary assembly logic.
Pre-arrival cabin, assistance, and trolley signals are synthetic but operationally grounded scenario inputs.
Failure probability, urgency class, and remaining-flights proxy are produced by locally trained models on synthetic reliability-oriented data.
Inventory risk combines a locally trained classifier with a transparent monotonic what-if guard; affected area and recommendation are transparent post-model operational logic.
Assistance readiness is intentionally implemented as dispatch-support logic rather than unsupported localization ML.
Dominant bottleneck and probability distribution are produced by a local multiclass model trained on process-aware synthetic turnaround data.
Fusion outputs are deterministic OCC coordination logic built on module outputs and scenario state.
Operator input changes deterministically recompute the synthetic scenario state before module inference and fusion are re-run locally.
Delay-risk and benefit indicators are demonstrator-level simulated impact values, not validated airline deployment outcomes.
This page is explanatory metadata documenting provenance, reproducibility, and limitations rather than a predictive module.

panel: Frontend fallback responses when backend is unavailable
note: These responses are deliberate resilience placeholders only and should not be used as evidence of model behavior.
